evidence.scientific_clarity: Force-directed layout with community colors is clearly legible; 4 communities are visually separable, but white node labels on small nodes are hard to read and the graph is somewhat dense.
evidence.typography: Script uses no pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype setting; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap-embedded) DejaVu Sans glyphs—not editable vector text—and font family is the matplotlib DejaVu Sans default.
evidence.palette: Custom 4-color qualitative palette (#E63946 red, #457B9D blue, #2A9D8F teal, #E9C46A yellow) avoids red/green directional misuse; reasonably CB-safe (no pure green/red pairing), but not formally validated against Okabe-Ito or ColorBrewer.
evidence.layout_density: Figure area is well-used for a single-panel network; k-scaling and 120 iterations give reasonable separation; no overlapping labels visible at 6.5pt bold white; modest whitespace margin is acceptable.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure contents; legend is inside the plot (upper-right) and unobtrusive; no axis labels needed for network; no numerical finding cited in the caption reduces scientific impact.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains Type 3 (rasterised) DejaVu Sans fonts—text is not selectable/editable; script does not set matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42 or 'svg.fonttype'=0, which is required for true vector text.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial (pdf 18 kB, png 100 kB, svg 50 kB, script 82 lines).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption describes algorithm (Fruchterman-Reingold, seed 42), edge alpha, and data source; however it contains no numerical finding (e.g., number of edges, modularity, largest community size) that a reader could verify.

overall_strengths: All required output files (pdf, png, svg, caption.tex, gen_figure.py) are present and non-trivial.
Qualitative palette avoids red/green directional misuse and the network layout cleanly separates the four communities with tuned spring-layout parameters.
overall_weaknesses: Font type is Type 3 (bitmap) because pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype=0 were not set, making text in the PDF non-selectable and violating the vector-fidelity requirement.
Caption lacks any numerical claim (e.g., edge count, community sizes, modularity score), and the default DejaVu Sans font is used throughout without specifying a publication-quality typeface.
